channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: We need a national STEM Act to stem the risk of a declining America
item.description: Wars have unintended consequences, and positive results can emerge from tragedy. For proof, just look at the list from NATO of some unexpected outcomes of R&D that led to society-changing benefits.
item.dc:creator: Frederic Bertley, Fast Company

item.title: SAP: good figures, but bad mood
item.description: There are rumblings among the SAP workforce: The index value for employee engagement is at a ten-year low. For 2024, SAP management around CEO Christian Klein expects only 70 to 74 percent. This is according to the current financial report for the third quarter of 2023.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CIOs must become chief IT evangelists
item.description: Technology evangelists abound in the IT sector, but typically the term is used to describe a marketing-related role aimed at promoting a product, service, or technology.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why Python is the language of choice for AI
item.description: The widespread adoption of AI is creating a paradigm shift in the software engineering world. Python has quickly become the programming language of choice for AI development due to its usability, mature ecosystem, and ability to meet the data-driven needs of AI and machine learning (ML) workflows.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Securing the digital future: How Huawei is tackling cybersecurity in the GCC
item.description: As the GCC countries continue to evolve into global digital hubs, the adoption of technologies such as 5G, AI, and IoT is accelerating rapidly. These advancements offer immense economic growth and innovation potential, but they also introduce new cybersecurity challenges.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cyber Resilience Act dell’UE: che cos’è e come faciliterà la vita ai CIO
item.description: Il Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) [in inglese] dell’Unione Europea ha avuto il via libera del Consiglio dei ministri dell’UE: si completa così il quadro normativo dell’Unione sulla cybersicurezza di cui fanno parte anche la NIS2 e, per il settore finanziario, la normativa DORA.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: FTC's rule banning fake online reviews goes into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal rule banning fake online reviews is now in effect. The Federal Trade Commission issued the rule in August banning the sale or purchase of online reviews.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Researchers say Arkansas may have 19M tons of lithium critical for battery power
item.description: Researchers said in a recent article that Arkansas may have 19 million tons of lithium, which is used in rechargeable batteries for important products like phones and electric cars.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft’s Copilot is adding AI agents for routine tasks
item.description: Microsoft will allow its customers to build autonomous artificial intelligence agents from next month, in its latest push to tap the booming technology amid growing investor scrutiny of its hefty AI investments.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Legislation now calling for spacecraft manufacturers to create cybersecurity plan when working with NASA
item.description: The Spacecraft Cybersecurity Act would require NASA to secure a cybersecurity protection. The post Legislation now calling for spacecraft manufacturers to create cybersecurity plan when working with NASA first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: Cuban says Harris campaign rejected live X interview with Musk
item.description: Billionaire investor Mark Cuban said Monday that Vice President Harris’s campaign rejected a live interview on the social platform X with billionaire Elon Musk, who owns the platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How Republicans pushed Silicon Valley social media companies to stop fighting election misinformation
item.description: The shift took place against the backdrop of an intimidation campaign led by Republican state and federal lawmakers aimed at forcing social media companies to platform falsehoods and hate speech and thwarting those working to study or limit the spread of that destabilizing content.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft to launch autonomous AI agents in November
item.description: Microsoft will soon let customers build autonomous AI agents that can be configured to perform complex tasks with little or no input from humans.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Built by one of their own, TradeZella lets day traders track and plan transactions
item.description: After a few years of successful day trading, Umar Ashraf found himself looking for software to plan, track, and evaluate his trades. But, he says, when he looked at the trade journaling products available about four years ago, he didn’t find any that fully met his needs.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: Artificial intelligence’s recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should be using the technology and worried about whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GoFundMe bets social media can unlock Gen Z giving. Meta partnership, new tools will test that
item.description: “We play a really important role helping people ask for help and give help in the world,” GoFundMe CEO Tim Cadogan said.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Floating energy platform could change way you get power in future
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how NoviOcean's hybrid platform uses wind, solar and wave power to generate clean energy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Big Tech turns to nuclear energy to power AI boom
item.description: Major technology companies are increasingly turning to nuclear energy as they attempt to keep up with the vast energy demands of artificial intelligence (AI) without derailing their highly publicized environmental goals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Cloud-enabled government: Seamless & secure data access
item.description: How is cloud adoption transforming federal agencies and driving mission success? The post Cloud-enabled government: Seamless & secure data access first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: AI isn’t really that smart yet, Apple researchers warn
item.description: While we wait for the Age Of Apple Intelligence, it may be worth considering a recent Apple research study that exposes critical weaknesses in existing artificial intelligence models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: WasmGC and the future of front-end Java development
item.description: JavaScript is the clear choice for front-end development today, but there is a longstanding push to be able to use Java on the front end. Among other things, Java brings a vast ecosystem of libraries, frameworks, and application code, which can be exposed via its APIs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Adobe starts roll-out of AI video tools, challenging OpenAI and Meta
item.description: Facing much larger rivals, Adobe has staked its future on building models trained on data that it has rights to use, ensuring the output can be legally used in commercial work.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Netskope extends data security with Dasera acquisition
item.description: Netskope today announced it has broadened its data protection capabilities by integrating data security posture management (DSPM) technology into its Netskope One Platform via its acquisition of Dasera.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Countries that have cashed in on their top-level domain names
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it's not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
